parsed.artifact_title: Non-Inferiority Conditions Note: Append-Only Baseline Comparison

claims.text: The protocol gates scheduler viability separately from artifact quality; the viability gate must be independently satisfied before any non-inferiority claim can proceed.
Artifact non-inferiority is assessed using a preregistered 0.10 margin measured against the append-only baseline mean quality score.
Observability is scored separately and cannot rescue an inferior artifact-quality result; the quality condition must be met independently of any observability outcome.

parsed.conclusion: Claiming non-inferiority to the append-only baseline requires the simultaneous satisfaction of three independent conditions: (1) the scheduler viability gate must be separately met per protocol; (2) the artifact quality score must clear the preregistered 0.10 non-inferiority margin against the append-only baseline mean; and (3) observability, scored separately, cannot compensate for an inferior artifact-quality result — the quality condition must hold on its own terms. All three must be true; failure of any one defeats the non-inferiority claim.
declared_losses: The actual append-only baseline quality mean is not yet known; this artifact states the conditions required for non-inferiority but does not adjudicate whether they are met.
parsed.recommended_action: Defer adjudication until the append-only baseline quality mean becomes available. At that point, evaluate the three conditions conjunctively: confirm scheduler viability independently, compare the artifact quality score to baseline + 0.10, and verify that the quality margin is met without reliance on observability scores.

carried_state.work_plan: Synthesize three constraints: (a) artifact quality must clear the 0.10 non-inferiority margin against the append-only baseline mean, (b) observability cannot compensate for an inferior quality result, and (c) scheduler viability is a separate gate. Produce a note stating the conjunction of conditions required to claim non-inferiority.

content.text: The protocol gates scheduler viability separately from artifact quality.
Artifact non-inferiority uses a preregistered 0.10 margin against append-only mean quality.
Observability is scored separately and cannot rescue an inferior artifact-quality result.

request_payload.instruction: Write the final artifact. Cite only evidence records that support claims. Do not cite distractors. If a limitation remains, include it in declared_losses.
request_payload.task: Write a note deciding what must be true before claiming non-inferiority to the append-only baseline.
